title: arctostaphylos glandulosa subsp. leucophylla
intro: Arctostaphylos glandulosa subsp. leucophylla, also known as the white-leaf manzanita, is a subspecies of evergreen shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Arctostaphylos glandulosa subsp. leucophylla are believed to include wound healing, immune system support, and anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, have been used to treat a range of ailments, including sore throats and respiratory issues.
